mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 3.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously once the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 3.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand and forward economic momentum, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires both series to show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions, or 20-day forward returns when both HOUST and RSAFS grow are not significantly higher than when only one grows (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative INDPRO signal, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 persons per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, indicating a weakening labor market momentum that precedes broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k and UNRATE unchanged or rising
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE change is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or 60-day forward returns in deceleration periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the Fed is in restrictive tightening mode. This dual condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress under higher discount rates and earnings pressure.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions, or 20-day forward returns during dual-tightening periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3.0% annualized, indicating robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal that operates whenever growth conditions are met.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 > 3.0% growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 2.0% year-over-year while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 150,000 persons per month, jointly signaling robust supply-side capacity expansion and labor productivity gains. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth > 2.0% and PAYEMS monthly growth > 150k
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong productivity gains
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions, or 20-day forward returns when both conditions are met are not significantly higher than when only one is met (t-test p > 0.05).
